feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Document and Reporting Compliance
feed.subtitle: SAP Document and Reporting Compliance blog posts in SAP Community

entry.title: Ask Me Anything with SAP Champions – Monthly Series (Session 2)
entry.content:
Hey dear SAP Community,

My name is Maria Antonieta Lopez,  I currently work as an SAP program director focusing on global SAP transformation programs, SAP S/4HANA implementations, enterprise architecture, governance frameworks, and digital innovation within the SAP ecosystem.

As an SAP Champion myself, I’ll be leading the March edition for the Ask Me Anything series, which we started together with my dear friend Sebastiano Marchesini.

So yes…

You can ask. Me. ANYTHING.

After 20+ years living inside SAP transformations, I’ve probably seen the good, the bad… and the “how did this system survive go-live?” moments. 😅

So over the next weeks I’ll be answering any question you have -- No filters - Just real SAP Live --.

💡We can explore topics like:

⚙️SAP S/4HANA implementations and global transformation programs. - How to successfully deliver end-to-end SAP S/4HANA implementations and large-scale transformation programs, enabling organizations to modernize their core business processes, standardize global operations, and adopt next-generation digital capabilities.

🏗️ SAP Enterprise Architecture. - Processes, artifacts, and strategies for TOGAF-based SAP enterprise architecture.

🧼 Clean Core in SAP Cloud ERP (Private & Public). - Project management processes for implementing or creating extensions or interfaces in your project. (BUILD for configurators and its use with Joule, pre-built APIs, newly developed APIs, and, of course, our platform-as-a-service, SAP Integration Suite, obviously within our beloved SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP).

☁️ RISE or GROW with SAP + Clean Core Governance & Methodologies. - From SAP Activate  to frameworks such as SAP Application Extension Methodology, Integration Solution Advisory Methodology, SAP Design Thinking, SAP Data Architecture Framework, and why not… even the SAP Business AI Framework.

We can talk about which format to use, or the process for something, or even… why I have to do this task.... and I'll tell you what will happen if you don't...

Muahaha! …💀 soo Yes… my SAP horror stories - like winter - are coming…

🏛️ How to build an SAP Center of Excellence (CoE) or provide an SAP AM services and actually 💙 live happily ever after with SAP 💙 using Cloud ALM + Focused Build + Activate.

🌍Globalization in a nutshell or by country. To comply locally and succeed globally... Long live SAP Document & Reporting Compliance! And our SAP partners' DRC extensions.

🧭 Solution roadmaps, etc.

And... if I don't know the answer? 👉 I promise I'll find someone who does... for you.

 

We have everything ready...  💬 Now it’s your turn.

Drop your question in the comments section 👇or You can ask anonymously here 👉 AMA Champion Anonymously Questions  or Scanning this QR 

 

This AMA edition will be open for questions until March 27, 2026.

After that, selected questions will be answered in a recorded video published on the SAP Community YouTube channel  👉https://www.youtube.com/@SAPCommunity 
A blog recap summarizing the questions and answers will also be published for the community.

Cuentame…

🤓What SAP topic should we explore together? ESPAÑOL or ENGLISH

entry.title: SAP Document and Reporting Compliance: Embedding Attachments in Belgium Peppol eInvoices
entry.content:
Introduction

Belgium's B2B eInvoicing mandate requires invoices to be transmitted via the Peppol network in UBL 2.1 format. Unlike some electronic document formats that support external document references, Peppol transmits the invoice as a single UBL XML file. This means there is no separate transmission channel for supporting documents — any attachment that needs to accompany the invoice must be embedded directly into the XML.

This blog post describes how to use the BAdI EDOC_ADAPTOR to read attachments from the GOS Attachment List of a billing document and embed them into the UBL XML, so they are transmitted together with the Peppol invoice or credit note.

Scope Note: This blog post covers the SAP S/4HANA on-premise implementation and S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition using BAdI EDOC_ADAPTOR. For SAP Public Edition, ABAP Environment, the equivalent BAdI is EDOC_ADAPTOR_CLOUD. The overall approach is similar, but backend APIs for attachment retrieval may differ and are not covered here.

How Attachments Are Embedded in UBL XML / BIS Billing 3.0

In UBL 2.1, supporting documents are referenced using the AdditionalDocumentReference element. For Peppol, the attachment content is Base64-encoded and placed directly inside the EmbeddedDocumentBinaryObject element. The following attributes are required:

  • mimeCode — the MIME type of the attached file (e.g., application/pdf, application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet)
  • filename — the name of the attached file

The DocumentTypeCode element with value 130 is used to indicate that the document is a supporting reference and no attachment, in line with EN16931 / BG-24.

A typical XML structure looks as follows:

<cac:AdditionalDocumentReference>
  <cbc:ID>42010AEF83EE1FE187BE825366FAF87F</cbc:ID>
  <cac:Attachment>
    <cbc:EmbeddedDocumentBinaryObject
      mimeCode="application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet"
      filename="Sample Timesheet.xlsx">
      [Base64-encoded content]
    </cbc:EmbeddedDocumentBinaryObject>
  </cac:Attachment>
</cac:AdditionalDocumentReference>

Since Peppol does not support external URI references as a transmission mechanism, embedding is the only viable approach to include attachments in a Peppol invoice.

Prerequisites

Functional / Configuration Side

  • The Belgium EU eInvoice scenario is configured and active. You can verify this in the Manage Electronic Documents app, where the billing document should appear under the Belgium EU eInvoice process.
  • The attachment has been uploaded to the GOS Attachment List of the relevant billing document (via Services for Object → Create → Create Attachment).
  • Keep individual attachments below 3 MB. While Peppol BIS Billing 3.0 does not impose a strict size limit, large embedded files can cause transmission issues. As a general guideline, keep the total embedded attachment size well below 3 MB.

ABAP / Developer Side

  • BAdI EDOC_ADAPTOR is available in the system.
  • A custom BAdI implementation will be created as described in the next section.
  • The relevant filter values are:
    • COUNTRY = BE
    • GENERIC_FILTER = (empty)

Implementation: BAdI EDOC_ADAPTOR

Create the BAdI Implementation

  1. Go to transaction SE18 and search for BAdI EDOC_ADAPTOR.
  2. Create a new implementation , e.g. ZEI_EDOC_ADAPTOR_BE.
  3. Set the filter values as follows:

Filter Value

COUNTRYBE
GENERIC_FILTER(leave empty)
  1. Navigate to method IF_EDOC_ADAPTOR~SET_OUTPUT_DATA and add the implementation described in the following sections.
  2. Activate the BAdI implementation.

Check Source Type and Retrieve the Billing Document

The first step inside the method is to verify that the source document is an SD billing document and to retrieve the billing document number. If the source type is not SD_INVOICE or the billing document number is initial, the method exits without further processing.

DATA: ls_sd_src_data TYPE edoc_src_data_sd_invoice.

" Only process SD Invoice source documents
IF io_source->mv_source_type = 'SD_INVOICE'.

  " Retrieve SD source data including the billing document header
  io_source->get_data( IMPORTING es_data = ls_sd_src_data ).

  " Only proceed if a valid billing document number is available
  IF ls_sd_src_data-document_header-vbeln IS NOT INITIAL.

    " ... (attachment retrieval and embedding — see below)

  ENDIF.

ENDIF.

Retrieve Attachments via cl_odata_cv_attachment_api

Attachments are retrieved using the cl_odata_cv_attachment_api class. This API reads all attachments stored in the GOS Attachment List for a given business object. In this case, the object type is VBRK (billing document header) and the object key is the billing document number.

DATA: lo_attachment_api TYPE REF TO cl_odata_cv_attachment_api,
      lt_attachments    TYPE if_odata_cv_attachment_api=>ty_t_attachment_content,
      ls_attachment     TYPE if_odata_cv_attachment_api=>ty_s_attachment_content.

" Get singleton instance of the attachment API
lo_attachment_api = cl_odata_cv_attachment_api=>get_instance( ).

" Retrieve all attachments for the billing document
TRY.
    lo_attachment_api->if_odata_cv_attachment_api~retrieve_attachments_content(
      EXPORTING
        iv_objecttype          = 'VBRK'
        iv_objectkey           = CONV objky( ls_sd_src_data-document_header-vbeln )
      IMPORTING
        et_attachment_contents = lt_attachments
    ).
  CATCH cx_root INTO DATA(lx_root).
    MESSAGE lx_root->get_text( ) TYPE 'I'.
    RETURN.
ENDTRY.

The result is an internal table lt_attachments containing one entry per attachment, including the file content, filename, and MIME type.

Filter Attachments (Optional)

By default, all attachments stored in the GOS Attachment List are retrieved and will be embedded into the XML. If you need to restrict which attachments are included — for example, to include only PDF files — you can filter the internal table before processing:

" Example: only include PDF attachments
DELETE lt_attachments WHERE mimetype NE 'application/pdf'.

Size Reminder: Before embedding, consider the total size of all attachments. As noted in the prerequisites, keep the combined embedded content below 3 MB to avoid potential transmission issues.

Embed Attachments into the UBL XML

The method handles two document types: invoices (UBL_INVOICE_TRANSM) and credit notes (UBL_CREDIT_NOTE_TRANSM). The correct path is determined by checking iv_interface_id. For each attachment, the following fields are mapped into the AdditionalDocumentReference structure of the UBL XML:

Attachment API Field UBL XML Element

file_idID
filenameDocumentDescription + filename attribute
mimetypemimeCode attribute
attachment_contentEmbeddedDocumentBinaryObject content

The DocumentTypeCode is set to 130 to indicate a document reference per EN16931 / BG-24.

Invoice path:

FIELD-SYMBOLS:
  <ls_out_invoice> TYPE edo_ubl_standard_business_doc2,
  <fs_attachment>  TYPE edo_ubl_document_reference.

          IF iv_interface_id = 'UBL_INVOICE_TRANSM'.
            ASSIGN cs_output_data TO <ls_out_struct_invoice>.
            IF <ls_out_struct_invoice> IS NOT ASSIGNED.
              RETURN.
            ENDIF.

            " Loop over all retrieved attachments and add them to the invoice
            LOOP AT lt_attachments INTO ls_attachment.
              APPEND INITIAL LINE TO <ls_out_struct_invoice>-invoice-additional_document_reference
                     ASSIGNING <fs_attachment>.

              " Populate attachment fields
              <fs_attachment>-id-base-base-content = ls_attachment-file_id.
              <fs_attachment>-attachment-embedded_document_binary_objec-base-content = ls_attachment-attachment_content.
              <fs_attachment>-attachment-embedded_document_binary_objec-base-filename = ls_attachment-filename.
              <fs_attachment>-attachment-embedded_document_binary_objec-base-mime_code = ls_attachment-mimetype.

              " Optional: Set document type code (e.g., '130' for attachment)
*              <fs_attachment>-document_type_code-base-base-content = '130'.
            ENDLOOP.

Credit Note path:

FIELD-SYMBOLS:
  <ls_out_crenote> TYPE edo_ubl_standard_business_doc5,
  <fs_attachment>  TYPE edo_ubl_document_reference.
          ELSEIF iv_interface_id = 'UBL_CREDIT_NOTE_TRANSM'.
            ASSIGN cs_output_data TO <ls_out_struct_crenote>.
            IF <ls_out_struct_crenote> IS NOT ASSIGNED.
              RETURN.
            ENDIF.

            " Loop over all retrieved attachments and add them to the credit note
            LOOP AT lt_attachments INTO ls_attachment.
              APPEND INITIAL LINE TO <ls_out_struct_crenote>-credit_note-additional_document_reference
                     ASSIGNING <fs_attachment>.

              " Populate attachment fields
              <fs_attachment>-id-base-base-content = ls_attachment-file_id.
              <fs_attachment>-attachment-embedded_document_binary_objec-base-content = ls_attachment-attachment_content.
              <fs_attachment>-attachment-embedded_document_binary_objec-base-filename = ls_attachment-filename.
              <fs_attachment>-attachment-embedded_document_binary_objec-base-mime_code = ls_attachment-mimetype.

              " Optional: Set document type code
*              <fs_attachment>-document_type_code-base-base-content = '130'.
            ENDLOOP.

          ENDIF.

Complete Source Code

METHOD if_edoc_adaptor~set_output_data.
      " ========================================================================
      " Retrieve and embed attachments for SD Invoice / Credit Note
      " Only executed when processing an SD billing document
      " ========================================================================

      DATA: ls_sd_src_data TYPE edoc_src_data_sd_invoice.

      " Check if this is an SD Invoice source
      IF io_source->mv_source_type = 'SD_INVOICE'.

        " Get the SD source data (billing document header and items)
        io_source->get_data( IMPORTING es_data = ls_sd_src_data ).

        " Only proceed if we have a valid billing document number
        IF ls_sd_src_data-document_header-vbeln IS NOT INITIAL.

          " ====================================================================
          " Step 1: Retrieve attachment IDs and content from GOS
          " ====================================================================
          DATA: lo_attachment_api TYPE REF TO cl_odata_cv_attachment_api,
                lt_attachments    TYPE if_odata_cv_attachment_api=>ty_t_attachment_content,
                ls_attachment     TYPE if_odata_cv_attachment_api=>ty_s_attachment_content.

          " Get singleton instance of the attachment API
          lo_attachment_api = cl_odata_cv_attachment_api=>get_instance( ).

          " Retrieve all attachments for the billing document
          " Object type = 'VBRK', Object key = billing document number (10 chars)
          TRY.
              lo_attachment_api->if_odata_cv_attachment_api~retrieve_attachments_content(
                EXPORTING
                  iv_objecttype  = 'VBRK'
                  iv_objectkey   = CONV objky( ls_sd_src_data-document_header-vbeln )
                IMPORTING
                  et_attachment_contents = lt_attachments
              ).
            CATCH cx_root INTO DATA(lx_root).
              " Log or handle error if attachment retrieval fails
              MESSAGE lx_root->get_text( ) TYPE 'I'.
              RETURN.
          ENDTRY.

          " ====================================================================
          " Step 2: Filter attachments (optional)
          " ====================================================================
          " TODO: Add your attachment filter logic here if needed.
          " For example, you may want to exclude certain file types or only
          " include attachments with a specific document type code.
          " Example:
          "   DELETE lt_attachments WHERE mime_type NE 'application/pdf'.
          " ====================================================================

          " ====================================================================
          " Step 3: Embed attachments into the UBL XML structure
          " ====================================================================
          FIELD-SYMBOLS:
            <ls_out_struct_invoice> TYPE edo_ubl_standard_business_doc2,
            <ls_out_struct_crenote> TYPE edo_ubl_standard_business_doc5,
            <fs_attachment>         TYPE edo_ubl_document_reference.

          " Determine if this is an Invoice or Credit Note
          IF iv_interface_id = 'UBL_INVOICE_TRANSM'.
            ASSIGN cs_output_data TO <ls_out_struct_invoice>.
            IF <ls_out_struct_invoice> IS NOT ASSIGNED.
              RETURN.
            ENDIF.

            " Loop over all retrieved attachments and add them to the invoice
            LOOP AT lt_attachments INTO ls_attachment.
              APPEND INITIAL LINE TO <ls_out_struct_invoice>-invoice-additional_document_reference
                     ASSIGNING <fs_attachment>.

              " Populate attachment fields
              <fs_attachment>-id-base-base-content = ls_attachment-file_id.
              <fs_attachment>-attachment-embedded_document_binary_objec-base-content = ls_attachment-attachment_content.
              <fs_attachment>-attachment-embedded_document_binary_objec-base-filename = ls_attachment-filename.
              <fs_attachment>-attachment-embedded_document_binary_objec-base-mime_code = ls_attachment-mimetype.

              " Optional: Set document type code rather than attachment
*              <fs_attachment>-document_type_code-base-base-content = '130'.
            ENDLOOP.

          ELSEIF iv_interface_id = 'UBL_CREDIT_NOTE_TRANSM'.
            ASSIGN cs_output_data TO <ls_out_struct_crenote>.
            IF <ls_out_struct_crenote> IS NOT ASSIGNED.
              RETURN.
            ENDIF.

            " Loop over all retrieved attachments and add them to the credit note
            LOOP AT lt_attachments INTO ls_attachment.
              APPEND INITIAL LINE TO <ls_out_struct_crenote>-credit_note-additional_document_reference
                     ASSIGNING <fs_attachment>.

              " Populate attachment fields
              <fs_attachment>-id-base-base-content = ls_attachment-file_id.
              <fs_attachment>-attachment-embedded_document_binary_objec-base-content = ls_attachment-attachment_content.
              <fs_attachment>-attachment-embedded_document_binary_objec-base-filename = ls_attachment-filename.
              <fs_attachment>-attachment-embedded_document_binary_objec-base-mime_code = ls_attachment-mimetype.

              " Optional: Set document type code rather than attachment
*              <fs_attachment>-document_type_code-base-base-content = '130'.
            ENDLOOP.

          ENDIF.

        ENDIF. " vbeln is not initial

      ENDIF. " source_type = SD_INVOICE

      " ========================================================================
      " Continue with any other custom logic below...
      " ======================================================================== 
 ENDMETHOD.

Result and Verification

Once the BAdI implementation is active, you can verify the end-to-end flow using the following steps.

Step 1: Add an Attachment to the Billing Document

Open the billing document in change mode (e.g., via VF02 or the Billing Documents app). Navigate to Services for Object → Create → Create Attachment and upload your file. The attachment will appear in the GOS Attachment List.

Step 2: Verify the Document in Manage Electronic Documents

Open the Manage Electronic Documents app and locate the billing document. The document should appear under the Belgium EU eInvoice process with status Created (In Process).

Step 3: Preview the XML

Select the document and choose Display/Preview XML. In the XML output, locate the AdditionalDocumentReference block. You should see:

  • The ID populated with the GOS file ID
  • The DocumentDescription containing the filename
  • The EmbeddedDocumentBinaryObject element with the correct mimeCode, filename, and Base64-encoded file content

 

Summary

In this blog post, we covered how to embed GOS attachments into a Belgium Peppol eInvoice XML using BAdI EDOC_ADAPTOR on SAP S/4HANA on-premise.

Key takeaways:

  • Peppol transmits invoices as a single UBL XML file — embedding is the only option for including attachments
  • Attachments can be retrieved from the GOS Attachment List using cl_odata_cv_attachment_api
  • The sample implementation handles both invoices (UBL_INVOICE_TRANSM) and credit notes (UBL_CREDIT_NOTE_TRANSM) via the iv_interface_id parameter
  • BAdI filter: COUNTRY = BE, GENERIC_FILTER = (empty)
  • Keep total embedded attachment size below 3 MB to avoid transmission issues
  • For SAP S/4HANA Cloud, ABAP Environment, use EDOC_ADAPTOR_CLOUD — backend APIs for attachment retrieval may differ

entry.title: Price Amount Recalculation in Peppol E-Invoicing for SAP
entry.content:
Introduction

In the world of electronic invoicing, especially within the Peppol network, precision is paramount. A single misplaced decimal can lead to rejected invoices, delayed payments, and frustrated business partners. One of the frequently discussed topics is the calculation of the PriceAmount field in UBL invoices. This blog post aims to shed light on why SAP's standard solution for Document and Reporting Compliance (e-invoicing) behaves the way it does, and how you can tailor it to your specific business needs.

Scope Note: This blog post covers the SAP S/4HANA on-premise implementation and S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition using BAdI EDOC_ADAPTOR, and assuming the latest version as per 2783795 - Electronic Document Processing for Peppol Countries/Regions: Implementation Overview - SAP for Me is implemented. 
This blog post refers only to eDocuments which were created out of Sales & Distribution Billing documents, as those allow price conditions as allowances or surcharges.
For SAP Public Edition, ABAP Environment, the equivalent BAdI is EDOC_ADAPTOR_CLOUD. The overall approach is similar, but backend APIs to format the amounts in the XML may differ and are not covered here.

As this behavior is cross-country enabled and as the mapping for CrossIndustryInvoice for Germany is implemented on top of the Peppol mapping, this is also valid for Germany / ZUGFeRD process as outlined here.

What is the Issue? The Peppol Rule PEPPOL-EN16931-R120

At the heart of the matter lies a critical Peppol validation rule: PEPPOL-EN16931-R120. This rule mandates that the Invoice line net amount must be consistent with the quantity and price. In simple terms, the following equation must hold true (within a defined rounding tolerance):

LineExtensionAmount = InvoicedQuantity * (PriceAmount / BaseQuantity)

If an invoice line includes discounts or surcharges (AllowanceCharge), the formula expands:

LineExtensionAmount = (InvoicedQuantity * (PriceAmount / BaseQuantity)) - Allowances + Charges

If this mathematical check fails, the Peppol Access Point will reject the invoice. The ultimate goal of the SAP standard solution is to ensure every generated invoice is compliant and successfully transmitted through the network.

Why SAP Cannot Deliver the Perfect Calculation in Every Case

The challenge arises because different businesses represent prices and discounts in different ways. SAP's e-invoicing solution must balance flexibility with the strict need for compliance.

SAP's standard mapping tries to generate a valid invoice that will be accepted by the Peppol Network. It does this by recalculating the PriceAmount if it detects a potential violation of the R120 rule. This often happens in two common scenarios:

  1. Hidden Discounts/Surcharges: Many businesses prefer not to show item-level discounts or surcharges separately in the final XML, at least not all of them. At the same time, they want the PriceAmount to reflect the net price. If the discount/surcharge conditions are not mapped to the <cac:AllowanceCharge> element in the XML, the standard PriceAmount (often derived from a gross price condition) will not match the final LineExtensionAmount. SAP's logic detects this and recalculates the PriceAmount to absorb these "hidden" values.

  2. High-Precision Prices: For low-priced items sold in bulk, kWh sold in Utilities, or items with prices derived from complex calculations, the calculated unit price can have many decimal places (e.g., €7.39933 per piece). The UBL standard allows for this, but historically, currency fields were often handled with only two decimal places. SAP's standard logic scales the price, but keeps two digits, avoiding rounding errors that would violate rule PEPPOL-EN16931-R120. So instead of showing 7.39933 / 1 piece, it might show 7399.33 / 1000 pieces. While mathematically identical, this can be confusing for human readers of the invoice.

This scaling logic is what customers may find "weird". However, it's a safe-by-default mechanism to guarantee compliance.

Tailoring the Calculation with a BAdI Implementation

The good news is that you have full control. Using the Business Add-In (BAdI) EDOC_ADAPTOR, you can implement your own logic to override the standard scaling behavior.

The best place to do this is in the BAdI method IF_EDOC_ADAPTOR~SET_OUTPUT_DATA. This method is called after SAP has performed its standard mapping, giving you the final data structure just before it's converted to XML.

Here is a sample implementation that rescales the price based on a simple rule: if the invoiced quantity is less than 1000, use a BaseQuantity of 1; otherwise, use 1000. This provides a more predictable and readable output, and can be fine-tuned to your needs.

CLASS zcl_edoc_adaptor_scaling IMPLEMENTATION.

  METHOD if_edoc_adaptor~set_output_data.
    " This BAdI implementation rescales the PriceAmount and BaseQuantity
    " according to a custom logic, overriding the standard SAP scaling.

    FIELD-SYMBOLS:
      <fs_invoice_data>   TYPE any,
      <lt_invoice_line>   TYPE STANDARD TABLE,
      <fs_line>           TYPE any,
      <fs_price>          TYPE any,
      <fs_price_amount>   TYPE any,
      <fs_base_quantity>  TYPE any,
      <fs_inv_quantity>   TYPE any.

    DATA:
      lv_current_price     TYPE p LENGTH 16 DECIMALS 10,
      lv_current_base_qty  TYPE p LENGTH 16 DECIMALS 10,
      lv_invoiced_quantity TYPE fklmg,
      lv_unit_price        TYPE p LENGTH 16 DECIMALS 10,
      lv_new_base_quantity TYPE p LENGTH 16 DECIMALS 10,
      lv_new_price_amount  TYPE p LENGTH 16 DECIMALS 10.

    IF iv_interface_id CS 'UBL_INVOICE_'.
      ASSIGN cs_output_data TO <fs_invoice_data>.
      IF <fs_invoice_data> IS NOT ASSIGNED. RETURN. ENDIF.

      ASSIGN COMPONENT 'INVOICE-INVOICE_LINE' OF STRUCTURE <fs_invoice_data> TO <lt_invoice_line>.
      IF <lt_invoice_line> IS NOT ASSIGNED. RETURN. ENDIF.

      LOOP AT <lt_invoice_line> ASSIGNING <fs_line>.
        " Get current Price, BaseQuantity, and InvoicedQuantity
        ASSIGN COMPONENT 'PRICE' OF STRUCTURE <fs_line> TO <fs_price>.
        ASSIGN COMPONENT 'INVOICED_QUANTITY-BASE-BASE-CONTENT' OF STRUCTURE <fs_line> TO <fs_inv_quantity>.
        IF <fs_price> IS NOT ASSIGNED OR <fs_inv_quantity> IS NOT ASSIGNED.
          CONTINUE.
        ENDIF.

        ASSIGN COMPONENT 'PRICE_AMOUNT-BASE-CONTENT' OF STRUCTURE <fs_price> TO <fs_price_amount>.
        ASSIGN COMPONENT 'BASE_QUANTITY-BASE-BASE-CONTENT' OF STRUCTURE <fs_price> TO <fs_base_quantity>.

        IF <fs_price_amount> IS NOT ASSIGNED OR <fs_base_quantity> IS NOT ASSIGNED.
          CONTINUE.
        ENDIF.
        
        lv_current_price = <fs_price_amount>.
        lv_current_base_qty = <fs_base_quantity>.
        lv_invoiced_quantity = <fs_inv_quantity>.

        IF lv_current_base_qty = 0.
          CONTINUE.
        ENDIF.

        " 1. Calculate the true unit price from the existing values
        lv_unit_price = lv_current_price / lv_current_base_qty.

        " 2. *** CUSTOMER-SPECIFIC SCALING LOGIC STARTS HERE ***
        IF lv_invoiced_quantity < 1000.
          lv_new_base_quantity = 1.
        ELSE.
          lv_new_base_quantity = 1000.
        ENDIF.
        " *** CUSTOMER-SPECIFIC SCALING LOGIC ENDS HERE ***

        " 3. Calculate the new PriceAmount based on the new BaseQuantity
        lv_new_price_amount = lv_unit_price * lv_new_base_quantity.

        " 4. Write the new values back and format for XML output
        <fs_price_amount>  = lv_new_price_amount.
        <fs_base_quantity> = lv_new_base_quantity.

        cl_edoc_map_aif=>format_negative_amount( CHANGING cv_amount = <fs_price_amount> ).
        cl_edoc_map_aif=>format_negative_amount( CHANGING cv_amount = <fs_base_quantity> ).

      ENDLOOP.
    ENDIF.
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

Summary

Navigating Peppol compliance requires a careful balance between standard processes and business-specific needs. While SAP's default behavior for PriceAmount recalculation prioritizes compliance with rule PEPPOL-EN16931-R120 to ensure your invoices are accepted, it may not always align with your desired representation.

Key Takeaways

  • Compliance is King: The primary reason for the recalculation is to comply with PEPPOL-EN16931-R120 and prevent invoice rejection.
  • You Have Choices: You can configure how discounts and surcharges appear. Either show them separately in <cac:AllowanceCharge> elements or absorb them into a net PriceAmount. The latter often triggers the recalculation.
  • You Have Control: The EDOC_ADAPTOR BAdI provides a powerful, upgrade-safe way to implement your own pricing representation logic, overriding the default scaling to produce invoices that are both compliant and easy for your customers to understand.

entry.title: Germany eInvoicing Mandate: Technical Pitfalls for SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud - and How to Avoid Them
entry.content:
Introduction

Germany is in the final stretch of a major regulatory shift in business-to-business invoicing. The legal foundation — the Wachstumschancengesetz and the amended §14 UStG — together with an EU derogation for mandatory B2B e-invoicing, sets out a clear and binding timeline:

Date Obligation

1 January 2025All German businesses must be able to receive EN 16931-compliant e-invoices (at least one of the following: XRechnung, ZUGFeRD 2.x, Peppol BIS 3.0)
Until 31 December 2026Paper and PDF may still be issued with the recipient's consent; reception duty already applies
1 January 2027Mandatory issuance of structured e-invoices for businesses whose prior-year revenue exceeded 800.000 EUR
1 January 2028Issuance obligation extends to all domestic B2B businesses, regardless of turnover

Only structured, machine-readable formats aligned with EN 16931 qualify as valid e-invoices. Pure PDFs — even sent by e-mail — and non-EN 16931 EDI formats no longer meet the legal requirement. This is also true for the good old INVOIC02 IDoc, by the way. The mandate applies to invoices exchanged between businesses VAT-established in Germany (Inland-zu-Inland).

For organisations running SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud or On-Premise, 2026 is the preparation year. The following four technical go-live pitfalls are among the most frequently encountered when setting up the SAP eDocument framework for Germany. Knowing them in advance can save your project significant time and frustration.

Before You Start: Align Finance, Tax, and IT

Technical configuration alone will not get you to a successful go-live. E-invoicing projects regularly stall or require expensive rework because the three key functions — Finance, Tax, and IT — operate in silos.

  • Finance owns the invoice lifecycle, approval workflows, and supplier relationships. They feel the pain first when processes break.
  • Tax carries the compliance responsibility. If mandatory fields such as VAT rates, buyer/seller data, or country-specific codes are missing, the invoice is legally invalid — regardless of whether the XML was transmitted successfully.
  • IT builds and operates the integration. But without clear, early requirements from Finance and Tax, they will build the wrong thing on time.

The pattern repeats across implementations: IT is halfway through the SAP eDocument setup, Tax steps in and discovers that several legally required fields are not being populated, and the project is delayed by weeks while mappings are redone and data models are extended. This is not a technology failure — it is a coordination failure.

What the best-prepared projects do differently:

  1. Form a joint steering group with representatives from all three functions from day one.
  2. Co-create requirements: map the full invoice lifecycle together, agree on which fields are legally mandatory versus operationally useful, and identify where master data gaps exist before configuration begins.
  3. Define shared success criteria: invoice validation rate, audit readiness, and mandate coverage — not just "went live on time."

For a deeper look at this cross-functional dynamic and how to build your organisation's e-invoicing team, see Fabio Santoro's article The E-Invoicing Dream Team: Why Finance, Tax, and IT Must Play as One.

With that organizational groundwork in place, the you are good to go and navigate the technical implementation. The pitfalls are outlined in this Blog Post.

Pitfall 1 — eDocument Is Never Created

Symptom

An SD billing document or FI invoice has been posted successfully, but the eDocument Cockpit (EDOC_COCKPIT) shows nothing. No eDocument appears, and nothing can be submitted.

Root Causes and Fixes

1.1 Missing Customizing — Assign eDocument Types

The eDocument framework must know which document types should produce an eDocument.

T003EDOCV — Assign eDocument Type to Accounting Document Type

This view is evaluated when the eDocument is created from the FI accounting document path (source type FI_INVOICE) or SD Billing document path (source type SD_INVOICE). It maps the accounting document type (e.g. DR customer invoice, DG credit memo) to an eDocument type. This covers:

  • Accounting documents posted directly in FI (e.g. transaction FB01)
  • The FI document created by the VF01 billing run, when the eDocument framework is also triggered from the FI event

Navigate to Customizing → Cross-Application Components → General Application Functions → Document and Reporting Compliance → Country/Region-Specific Settings → Germany → Electronic Document Processing → General Settings → Assign eDocument Type to Accounting Document Type.

Decision at a glance

Trigger path Source type in framework View evaluated

SD billing document (VF01) with Accounting DocumentSD_INVOICET003EDOCV
FI accounting document (FB01 or FI transfer from SD)FI_INVOICET003EDOCV
Pro forma / billing type without accounting docSD_INVOICEnot supported for Peppol solution / ZUGFeRD

💡Note for S/4HANA Public Cloud (Cloud Edition) readers

  • TVFKEDOCV is the only relevant view for SD Billing Documents in Public Cloud.
  • T003EDOCV is irrelevant for the SD_INVOICE source path in Public Cloud — only the FI_INVOICE path still uses it.

If you are running Public Cloud and your SD Billing Documents are not generating eDocuments, check TVFKEDOCV first.

💡Practical guidance for Germany (Private Cloud / On-Premise): For the typical German B2B invoice triggered via the SD billing flow (VF01) or direct FI postings (FB01), maintain T003EDOCV.

➡️Reference: Assigning eDocument Type to Accounting Document Type | SAP Help Portal

1.2 Source Document Types Not Activated for the Company Code

After defining the eDocument types, the Source Document Types must be activated per company code. Without this step, no eDocument will be created even if the type assignment is correct.

➡️Reference: Activating Source Type Documents for Company Code | SAP Help Portal

1.3 Interface Type Not Defined for the eDocument

Each eDocument type requires an interface type definition that determines how the document is transmitted. For Germany with Peppol Network Exchange, this points to the appropriate SOA service and logical port.

➡️Reference: Defining Interface Type (Peppol) | SAP Help Portal

➡️Reference: Defining Interface Type (ZUGFeRD) | SAP Help Portal

1.4 Customer Not Maintained in EDOEUBUPAV

The most common single cause of missing eDocuments: the customer of the invoice has not been registered in the business partner Peppol registration view.

  • Execute transaction SM30, enter view EDOEUBUPAV, and click Maintain.
  • Add the customer number, their Peppol participant identifier type (e.g. VAT number, Leitweg-ID), and the identifier value.
  • Without this entry, the system cannot determine the Peppol recipient and will not create the eDocument.

➡️Reference: Maintaining Organisation Registration Number for Business Partners | SAP Help Portal

💡Productivity Tip — Discover Peppol-Registered Business Partners Automatically

Rather than emailing each business partner to ask for their Peppol ID, use the SAP-delivered report "List Business Partners for Peppol Exchange of Electronic Documents" (transaction EDOEUBUPAV-lookup, implemented via SAP Note 3350153).

The report scans your business partner master data (VAT numbers, Leitweg-IDs, KVK numbers, etc.), matches them against the live Peppol network directory, and returns a list showing which partners are already registered. Business partners that are Peppol participants but not yet enabled in your system can be activated with a single click — no manual data collection needed.

➡️Implement via: SAP Note 3350153

1.5 Retrigger eDocument Creation

Once customizing is corrected, previously missed invoices can be processed retroactively:

  1. Execute EDOC_COCKPIT.
  2. Next to Variant, click Change.
  3. Select the appropriate tab (e.g. Billing Documents for SD Billing Documents).
  4. Check "Not yet created".
  5. Execute the search. In the results, identify entries marked as Relevant.
  6. Select them and click Create.

Pitfall 2 — Outbound Communication Failures (HTTP 401, 404, or Wrong Logical Port)

Symptom

When submitting an eDocument, the eDocument Cockpit or the Manage Electronic Documents Fiori app returns errors such as:

  • HTTP Code 401: Unauthorized
  • HTTP Code 404: Not Found
  • SOAP:1007 SRT: Unsupported xstream found
  • A logon popup appears unexpectedly
  • No communication is triggered at all

Root Causes and Fixes

2.1 Utilize SRT_UTIL

To diagnose the issue, we need to trace the outgoing web service call using the SRT_UTIL transaction. Here’s how to use SRT_UTIL to capture and analyze the outgoing request payload and URL:

Step-by-Step Usage of SRT_UTIL:

  1. Access SRT_UTIL:

    • Log into your SAP system using SAP GUI.
    • Run transaction code SRT_UTIL.

  2. Configure Payload Trace:

    • Right-click on "Users & Terminals & Request URI" and choose "Add User, Terminal or Request URI".
    • Preferably select the option "User Name" to trace by the current User.
    • Go to the "Configuration" tab, set "Payload Trace" to "Active", and save the configuration.

  3. Activate Trace and Reproduce Issue:

    • Switch to the "Payload Trace" tab.
    • Execute the process that triggers the outgoing web service call from eDocument Cockpit. Requests and responses will now be captured.

  4. View Outgoing Requests:

    • The relevant log entries will appear under the "Payload Trace" tab.
    • Entries are listed per step, with columns showing type (Request, Response), timestamp, and operation name. You can filter or search entries for easier analysis.

  5. Get Outgoing Request Payload:

    • Double-click a specific log entry (of type "Request") for the outgoing call.
    • In the detail view, click the "Original XML" button. This opens the raw XML payload being sent out.

  6. Get Outgoing Request URL:

    • In the same detail view, inspect the "Request URI" or similar fields; this shows the full endpoint URL to which the outgoing request was sent.
    • The logs often include user, operation, URI, and payload details.

Tips:

  • You can trace by specific user, terminal, or operation if needed for more targeted troubleshooting.
  • Be careful about enabling payload trace in live/production environments, as it can generate large volumes of data and potentially impact system performance.
  • Tracing is usually limited in duration (typically 2 hours), after which it must be reactivated if further analysis is necessary.
  • For outbound messages, payloads and URLs are recorded for full SOAP/REST requests.

Summary Table: How to Retrieve Key Info in SRT_UTIL

Information How to Retrieve

Payload (XML)Double-click relevant request entry, click "Original XML"
Outgoing URLView "Request URI" field/details in entry or detail view

By following these steps in SRT_UTIL, you can reliably trace outgoing web service requests from SAP, examine the payload (XML body) sent, and see the destination URL for each call.

2.2. Check your BC-Sets

Especially when no communication can be traced after submitting the eDocument, verify the installation of the BC-Sets from Notes 2440686, 2010697 and 2360094.
Also check Activating Business Configuration (BC) Set to Enable Invoice Response | SAP Help Portal.

2.3 SM30 → EDOSOASERV: The Bridge Between eDocument and SOAMANAGER

View EDOSOASERV is the critical link that tells the eDocument framework which SOAMANAGER logical port to call for each service. It is maintained via SM30 or via Customizing → Cross-Application Components → General Application Functions → Document and Reporting Compliance → Country/Region-Specific Settings → Germany → Electronic Document Processing → General Settings → Define SOA Services for Communication.

Required entries for Germany (Peppol / XRechnung outbound):

SOA Service Name Logical Port Purpose

EU_INVOICE_TRANSMEDO_UBL_SEND_INVOICE_SERV_PORTOutbound invoice submission
EU_CR_NOTE_TRANSMEDO_UBL_SEND_CRED_NOTE_SERV_PORTOutbound credit note submission
EU_APP_RESP_TRANSMEDO_UBL_APPLICATION_RESPONSEOutgoing invoice response to business partner
EU_GENERIC_WEBSERVEDO_EU_GENERIC_WEBSERVHTML display (eDocument Cockpit / Manage Electronic Documents)
EU_EMAIL_PREPAREEDO_EU_GENERIC_WEBSERVCross-Industry-Invoice validation validation (Customer Invoice via E-Mail)

Important rules:

  • If the Company Code field is left empty, the entry acts as the default for all company codes not covered by a more specific entry.
  • If a specific company code is entered, the entry applies only to that company code.
  • The logical port name here must exactly match the name configured in SOAMANAGER
  • The corresponding logical port in SOAMANAGER must point to the correct proxy object (e.g. CO_EDO_UBL_SEND_INVOICE_V1_0 for invoice submission, CO_DCS_DCP_GENERIC_WEBSERVICE for HTML display).

➡️Reference: Defining SOA Services for Electronic Customer Invoices| SAP Help Portal

➡️Reference: Defining SOA Services for Electronic Customer Invoices (Submission by E-Mail)| SAP Help Portal

➡️Reference: Creating Logical Ports in SOA Management | SAP Help Portal

2.4 Wrong URL Path in the Logical Port

The URL path in each logical port must point to the correct environment endpoint:

Environment Path

Test/cxf/PeppolSendDocumentTest
Production/cxf/PeppolSendDocumentProd

Other settings: Protocol = HTTPS, Port = 443, URL type = URL components.

2.5 Incorrect Authentication

S/4HANA Private Cloud / On-Premise supports two authentication methods against SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, Cloud Edition:

  • X.509 Client Certificate: The client certificate must be signed by a Certificate Authority listed in SAP Note 2801396. Export the certificate from transaction STRUST (without private key, PEM format, no whitespace), and paste it into the service binding in the SAP BTP Cockpit.

  • OAuth 2.0 (recommended): OAuth 2.0 credentials do not expire and do not require a client certificate signed by a specific trusted CA. Configure via transaction OA2C_CONFIG. The OAuth client ID, secret, and token endpoint URL are obtained from the service binding in SAP BTP.

    ➡️Reference: OAuth 2.0 Client Authentication | SAP Help Portal

Tip: If you are starting fresh, prefer OAuth 2.0. Certificates expire and require renewal; OAuth 2.0 credentials do not.

2.6 Testing the Connection

In SOAMANAGER, use Ping Web Service on your logical port. A response of:

  • HTTP 405 (Method Not Allowed) = connection is working (the service does not support HTTP HEAD, but the network path is reachable)
  • HTTP 200 (OK) = connection is working
  • HTTP 401 (Unauthorized) = authentication misconfiguration → review Section 2.4
  • SSL handshake error = trust issue → see Pitfall 3

Pitfall 3 — SSL Handshake Fails: SSSLERR_PEER_CERT_UNTRUSTED

Symptom

When submitting an eDocument or pinging the web service, the system throws an error like:

SSL handshake with ...:443 failed: SSSLERR_PEER_CERT_UNTRUSTED (-102)
Peer's X.509 certificate (chain) validation failed (missing trust?)

Root Cause

The server's root certificate — the topmost certificate in the chain presented by the SAP DRC service endpoint — has not been imported into the certificate trust list of the SAP system's PSE in transaction STRUST.

Solution

Step 1 — Retrieve the root certificate via your browser

  1. Open the service binding URL (e.g. https://peppol-cf-cust.it-cpi018-rt.cfapps.eu10-003.hana.ondemand.com) in a web browser.
  2. Click the padlock icon in the address bar → View Certificate (or Certificate Information).
  3. Navigate to the Certification Path tab.
  4. Select the topmost (root) certificate in the chain.
  5. Export / download it to your local machine.

Step 2 — Import the root certificate into STRUST

  1. Execute transaction STRUST.
  2. Select the PSE used by the logical port (typically SSL Client (Standard)).
  3. Click Display.
  4. Click Import Certificate and select the downloaded root certificate file.
  5. Click Add to Certificate List.
  6. Save.

Once the root CA is trusted, SAP will trust all certificates issued by that authority — you do not need to import intermediate certificates separately.

For OAuth 2.0 users: also apply the same steps for the token endpoint host (typically https://peppol-cf-cust.authentication.eu10.hana.ondemand.com), which may use a different certificate chain.

For client certificate users: ensure your client certificate is issued by a CA from SAP Note 2801396. The SAP BTP side validates the client certificate against this list.

➡️Reference: Adding Server Root Certificate to Certificate Trust List | SAP Help Portal

Pitfall 4 — HTML Display Fails in eDocument Cockpit

Symptom

After creating an eDocument, clicking Display HTML in EDOC_COCKPIT produces:

No Interface is defined for process step DISPL_HTML (Message no. EDOCUMENT034)

Or the display call fails silently / returns an authentication error.

Root Causes and Fixes

4.1 Missing Logical Port for HTML Display Service

A separate logical port is required for the HTML rendering service. Create logical port EDO_EU_GENERIC_WEBSERV in SOAMANAGER for the service CO_DCS_DCP_GENERIC_WEBSERVICE.

➡️Reference: Defining SOA Services for Communication | SAP Help Portal

4.2 Authentication Mismatch (Test vs. Production)

The service binding and the credentials configured in SOAMANAGER / OA2C_CONFIG must correspond to the same environment (test or production). Mixing test credentials with a production URL — or vice versa — results in authentication failures that manifest as display errors.

Checklist:

  • Service binding URL in the logical port → matches the intended environment?
  • OAuth 2.0 client credentials in OA2C_CONFIG → sourced from the same service binding?
  • Client certificate in STRUST → exported from the correct service binding?

Pitfall 5 — eDocument Stuck at "Received by Sender Access Point" (Yellow Indicator)

Symptom

After an invoice is submitted to SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, Cloud Edition (via Peppol or E-Mail scenario), the eDocument Cockpit shows the status "Received by sender access point" or "Request Acknowledged" — indicated by a yellow triangle — and the document does not progress further. A corresponding red entry may appear in the Document Monitor (Peppol Exchange app on BTP).
Likewise, incoming invoices may not show up in your Backend system, even though they were successfully received by the DRC Peppol Service.

This means the document was received and queued by SAP DRC in the cloud, but the push-back response to the SAP backend could not be delivered - either because the inbound channel from BTP to your S/4HANA system is misconfigured, or because the backend cannot correctly process the incoming response.

Root Causes and Fixes

Please check your Peppol Document Monitor App in BTP and try to access the error message from the list.

5.1 Cloud Connector Not Reachable or Not Connected (HTTP 503)

Error pattern:

Server returned HTTP response code: 503 for URL: http://<host>:<port>/sap/bc/srt/rfc/sap/dcppushservice/.../dcppushservice/pushrequest
Error Stream: There is no SAP Cloud Connector (SCC) connected to your subaccount matching the requested tunnel ...

or:

Timed out waiting for tunnel to open for tunnelId account:///...

Cause: The SAP Cloud Connector is not running, not connected to the BTP subaccount, or the tunnel for the backend system has not been established.

Resolution:

  1. Verify that the SAP Cloud Connector is running and connected: in the BTP Cockpit, under Connectivity → Cloud Connectors, the Cloud Connector should appear as Connected.
  2. Review the Cloud Connector subaccount configuration: ➡️ Creating Subaccount in Cloud Connector | SAP Help Portal ➡️ Cloud Connector | SAP Help Portal
  3. Verify the resource mapping (the backend URL path must be exposed in the Cloud Connector): ➡️ Adding Resource to Connection | SAP Help Portal
  4. Check whether the Cloud Connector's subaccount certificate has expired: ➡️ SAP Note 2950537 — Subaccount certificate expired in SAP Cloud Connector
  5. Also review firewall settings — the Cloud Connector must be able to reach the BTP endpoint outbound.
  6. Confirm the BTP Destination connection test passes in the Destinations app in BTP.

5.2 Company Code Cannot Be Determined for the Peppol Participant

Error pattern (English):

ERROR: Determination of the company code for participants <scheme>:<id> not possible

Error pattern (German):

Ermittlung des Buchungskreises für Beteiligten <scheme>:<id> nicht möglich

Cause: The good thing is - if you see this message, the connection was successful. The bad thing is - when SAP DRC pushes the an incoming Invoice back, the backend must map the Peppol Sender Participant ID to a company code. If this mapping is ambiguous or missing, the push response will be rejected.

Resolution — step by step:

  1. Identify the participant identifier scheme. Check whether the prefix corresponds to a VAT number (e.g. scheme 9930 is a German VAT ID; see the Peppol Participant Identifier Schemes list).

  2. If the scheme is a VAT number (e.g. 9930):

    • Open transaction OBY6 (company code global settings) and verify that exactly one company code is linked to the VAT number shown in the Sender Participant ID.
    • Also check table T001, field STCEG in SE16 for duplicate VAT entries across company codes.
    • If multiple company codes share the same VAT number, consider switching to GLN-based Peppol IDs to avoid ambiguity. ➡️ Background: Peppol IDs in the context of SAP Document Compliance | SAP Blogs

  3. If the scheme is not a VAT number (e.g. GLN or other):

    • Check table T001Z, field PAVAL in SE16 for duplicate company code parameter entries.
    • Again, duplicates here prevent unambiguous mapping.
  4. Custom extension for Company Code determination
    • If none of the above is feasible, consider a customer implementation.

5.3 Provider Service Not Configured or Misconfigured (HTTP 500 / 401 / 403)

Error patterns:

Server returned HTTP response code: 500 ...
"No Web service configuration for this access path: /sap/bc/srt/rfc/sap/dcppushservice/.../dcppushservice/pushrequest"

or HTTP 401 on the push URL, or:

Server returned HTTP response code: 403 ...
Access denied to resource / on system <host>:<port>. In case this was a valid request, ensure to expose the resource correctly in your cloud connector.

(Note: when only the host is shown in the error — without a path — the full URL path is missing from the BTP Destination.)

Cause: The SAP backend was reached, but the inbound SOAP web service for receiving push responses (dcppushservice) has not been activated or is misconfigured.

Resolution:

  1. Configure the inbound provider service in the SAP backend: ➡️ Configuring Provider Service | SAP Help Portal
  2. Verify the transport protocol setting: the protocol configured in the provider service (HTTP vs. HTTPS) must match the Transport Level Security setting in the Cloud Connector resource mapping. They must be in sync.
  3. Verify the BTP Destination URL includes the full path to the provider service — not just the hostname. A missing path causes 403 errors.
  4. Check the communication user's credentials and ensure the user account is not locked: ➡️ Setting Up Connectivity | SAP Help Portal

5.4 BTP Destination URL Has No Path (HTTP Reaches Host Only)

Error pattern:

java.io.IOException: http://<host>:443 | Error Stream: Service cannot be reached

Cause: The BTP Destination is configured with only the hostname, without a path to the provider web service.

Resolution: Update the BTP Destination URL to include the full path to the dcppushservice provider service as documented: ➡️ Adding Resource to Connection | SAP Help Portal

Rule: Always provide a valid path to the web service endpoint in the BTP Destination URL field, not just the host. Refer to the Setting Up Connectivity | SAP Help Portal for the exact URL structure.

5.5 Authentication Error on the Push URL (HTTP 401)

Error pattern:

Server returned HTTP response code: 401 for URL: http://<host>:<port>/sap/bc/srt/rfc/sap/dcppushservice/...

Cause: The communication user configured in the BTP Destination has incorrect credentials or is locked.

Resolution:

  1. Verify the username and password in the BTP Destination match a valid, unlocked SAP backend user.
  2. Test the user credentials directly by calling http://<internal-host>/sap/bc/ping?sap-client=<client> from a browser inside your network — authentication should succeed.
  3. Verify the full provider service URL is also reachable with those credentials.

5.6 Destination ID Exceeds 10-Character Limit

Error pattern (visible in the Peppol Document Monitor):

"Peppol_Backend_Connection-S4" contravenes the maxLength facet "10"

Cause: The BTP Destination name used to identify the backend system is longer than the 10-character maximum enforced by DRC.

Resolution: Rename the BTP Destination to a value with 10 characters or fewer, and update all references accordingly. ➡️ Setting Up Connectivity | S/4HANA or ERP

5.7 No Error Shown — Push Response Received but Not Processed

If no error is visible in the eDocument Cockpit and the yellow status persists, the push response may have reached the SAP backend but stalled in the inbound message queue.

Check:

  1. Execute transaction EDOC_INBOUND_MSG.
  2. Search for pending messages using List Existing Messages.
  3. Set Message Type to DE_PUSH_RESPONSE (for outgoing Invices via Peppol) or DE_BUSLVLRESP_PUSH (for incoming Application Responses via Peppol) or DE_CII_PUSH (for E-Mail scenarios) or DE_MESSAGE_PUSH (for incoming Invoices).
  4. Process any pending messages to advance the eDocument status.
  5. If transaction EDOC_INBOUND_MSG shows no messages at all - verify your Participant's Destination and make sure it points to your S/4HANA BTP Destination.

Summary Checklist

Use this quick-reference table before and during go-live preparation:

# Symptom Most Likely Cause Key Action

1aeDocument not created — no entry in EDOC_COCKPITMissing T003EDOCV customizingAssign eDocument Type to document types; activate per company code
1beDocument not created — no entry in EDOC_COCKPITCustomer missing in EDOEUBUPAVMaintain Peppol ID in SM30 → EDOEUBUPAV; use Note 3350153 report
2aHTTP 401 on submissionWrong authentication setupConfigure OAuth 2.0 or X.509 client cert correctly in SOAMANAGER
2bHTTP 404 or wrong endpointIncorrect logical port name or URL pathVerify logical port names and paths against SAP Help documentation
3SSSLERR_PEER_CERT_UNTRUSTED on any callServer root cert not trusted in STRUSTDownload root cert from browser; import into STRUST PSE trust list
4HTML display authentication errorMissing / wrong logical port for CO_DCS_DCP_GENERIC_WEBSERVICECreate EDO_EU_GENERIC_WEBSERV in SOAMANAGER
5aYellow status "Received by sender access point"Cloud Connector not connected or firewall blockingVerify Cloud Connector status; check BTP Destination connection test
5bYellow status + company code error in logAmbiguous Peppol participant ID → company code mappingCheck OBY6 / T001 / T001Z for duplicates
5cYellow status + HTTP 500 or 403 on push URLProvider service not configured or path missing in DestinationFollow Configuring Provider Service guide; add full path to BTP Destination
5dYellow status + HTTP 401 on push URLCommunication user locked or wrong credentialsVerify user in BTP Destination; test with /sap/bc/ping
5eDestination name too long error in cloud logBTP Destination name > 10 charactersRename Destination to ≤ 10 characters

Closing Thoughts

The German eInvoicing mandate is real, the deadlines are fixed, and the technical setup on SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud involves several layers — customizing, SOA connectivity, certificate management, business partner maintenance, and the inbound push-response channel — that must all be correct simultaneously. The pitfalls described above account for a large share of the support cases seen in practice.

The earlier you start, the more time you have to discover and address your specific configuration gaps. And with tools like the Peppol business partner lookup report (SAP Note 3350153), even the time-consuming task of identifying your Peppol-capable customer base can be automated.

entry.title: Extend SAP Document and Reporting Compliance Based Generic Cash Flow Statements
entry.content:
Cash flow statements are essential for every business, revealing the actual cash movements behind your financial numbers. While SAP S/4HANA already delivers country-specific cash flow reports for many regions, organizations in countries not yet covered — or those needing company-specific customizations — require a way to build their own. That is exactly what the Extension Guide for SAP Document and Reporting Compliance (DRC) Based Generic Cash Flow Statements addresses.

This guide, available for both SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and On-Premise/Private Cloud, provides a step-by-step walkthrough for extending the SAP-delivered generic cash flow statement to meet your specific country or company requirements. In this blog post, we provide a concise introduction to help you understand the guide's purpose, the key concepts behind it, and what to expect when you follow the extension process.

1. Background: Cash Flow Statements and the Direct Method Challenge

A cash flow statement tracks cash receipts and payments over a specific period, categorized into three activities: operating (core business), investing (long-term assets), and financing (debt, equity, dividends). Accounting standards allow two methods for presenting the operating section.

The indirect method starts with net income and adjusts for non-cash items and working capital changes. It is easier to prepare but does not show actual cash transactions. The direct method reports actual cash receipts and payments — more intuitive and better for forecasting, but significantly harder to implement. The main challenge is that traditional accounting systems record transactions by account, not by cash flow category. To prepare a direct method statement, you must identify cash transactions, classify each into the right category, and match receipts with payments.

This is where Offsetting Account Determination (OAD) becomes essential. OAD automatically pairs each debit posting with exactly one credit posting in your accounting documents, enabling the system to classify cash flows by their true nature. For example, when a supplier invoice is paid, OAD creates paired relationships that let the system trace exactly what each cash outflow represents — turning an otherwise manual process into an automated one.

2. SAP DRC and the Generic Cash Flow Statement

SAP Document and Reporting Compliance (DRC) — formerly known as Advanced Compliance Reporting (ACR) — is SAP's unified framework for statutory reporting. It provides a single platform to define, configure, execute, and submit regulatory reports across countries and regions.

Within DRC, SAP delivers several generic reports that serve as ready-to-use templates: Generic VAT, Generic Cash Flow Statements, Balance Sheet/Income Statement, and Generic Withholding Tax. The Generic Cash Flow Statement supports both the direct and indirect methods and already powers country-specific reports for 17+ countries including China, Argentina, Turkey, South Korea, Poland, Portugal, Indonesia, and more — with the list continuously expanding.

You can leverage the generic cash flow statement in three ways:

  • Use as-is: Simply assign the delivered report definition to your reporting entity and start generating reports
  • Extend with reference: Create a new report definition that inherits from the generic version, then customize parameters, queries, or event handlers to fit your needs
  • Copy and modify: Duplicate the generic definition entirely and adapt it for countries or scenarios requiring deeper customization

The Extension Guide focuses on the second approach — extending with reference — which provides the best balance of reusability and flexibility.

3. How to Extend: A High-Level Walkthrough

The Extension Guide walks you through a five-step process to create your own DRC-based cash flow statement report. Here is a high-level overview of each step:

Step 1: Preparation — Ensure you have the required business roles assigned. You will need an Administrator role (for configuring report definitions and categories), a Developer role (for creating ABAP classes in your development tenant), and an Accountant role (for running and submitting reports).

Step 2: Create ABAP Classes — In your development environment, create a custom event handler class that references the SAP-delivered generic class. This class handles parameter validation for the report's selection screen. The guide provides sample code you can adapt for your country.

Step 3: Define Report Definition and Category — Using the Define Statutory Reports app, create a new report definition by extending the generic version. The system uses an inheritance model — you can keep inherited values, redefine them, or add new ones. Then create a report category that groups your report with its phases, organizational units, and reporting activities.

Step 4: Configure — Set up a reporting entity through the Setting Up Your Statutory Reporting configuration. This includes assigning your report category, defining periodicity, and configuring cash flow statement versions, field sets for grouping, and offsetting account determination versions.

Step 5: Run and Submit — Execute your new report using the Run Statutory Reports app. You can preview the generated cash flow statement, export it to Excel, and update the submission status to mark the reporting period as complete.

The Extension Guide provides detailed screenshots and instructions for every step, making it possible to complete the entire process even if this is your first time working with DRC.

4. What's Next: Resources and Getting Started

Ready to get started? Here are the resources to guide you through the process:

  • Extension Guide for SAP DRC-Based Generic Cash Flow Statements (PDF) — The complete 25-page step-by-step guide with screenshots, sample code, and configuration instructions
  • Step-by-Step Implementation Guide: SAP DRC Cash Flow Statements — A comprehensive blog with 6 video tutorials covering everything from cash flow statement concepts to real-world configuration examples
  • Extensibility and Reuse in Statutory Reporting (SAP Help Portal) — Official documentation on extending and creating DRC reports

We recommend beginning with the video tutorials from the implementation blog to build a solid understanding of the DRC cash flow solution, and then using the Extension Guide for the hands-on extension work. If you have questions or feedback, feel free to leave a comment below or reach out through the SAP Community.

entry.title: SII Tip for Spain #10: Error 1166 in Canary Islands and Regime 15
entry.content:
In SII Tip #7, we covered Error 1166 ("El valor del campo TipoImpositivo no está incluido en la lista de valores permitidos") . In this follow-up post, we dive into a very specific — and commonly misunderstood — scenario that triggers the same error: incoming invoices from retailer companies in the Canary Islands under Régimen 15.

 

Background: The Canary Islands Fiscal Regime

The Canary Islands, while part of Spain and the European Union, operate under a distinct economic and fiscal framework. Due to their geographical location as an outermost region of the EU, the scarcity of natural resources, and their distance from the European continent, the islands have their own indirect taxation system.

The key difference: Value Added Tax (VAT) does not apply in the Canary Islands. Instead, supplies of goods and services are subject to the General Indirect Canary Tax (IGIC — Impuesto General Indirecto Canario).

The valid IGIC tax rates are defined in the article 51 of the Law 4/2012. In 2026, the applicable rates are:

 

IGIC RateDescription
0%Zero rate (tipo cero)
3%Reduced rate (tipo reducido)
5%Reduced rate
7%General rate (tipo general)
9.5%Increased rate
15%Special increased rate
20%Special rate (tobacco)

When the Canary Islands Tax Agency validates SII submissions, it checks that the <TipoImpositivo> field contains one of these accepted values. Any other value triggers Error 1166.

 

Incoming invoices from retailers in the Canary Islands — Regime 15

Under Canary Islands tax law, purchases from retailers (comerciantes minoristas) are exempted according to article 27 of the Real Decreto 1/2025 of the Canary Islands, however the buyer can deduct the implicit tax amount for the corresponding transactions, provided that the supplier states in the invoice the status of retailer company (“Comerciante Minorista”).

 

What is the Implicit Tax Charge (Carga Impositiva Implícita)?

The calculation of the implicit tax amount is defined in the.Article 29.3 of the IGIC Law (Ley 20/1991)

When a company purchases goods from a retailer company, the buyer can deduct an implicit tax charge (carga impositiva implícita) calculated using a coefficient K:

The implicit tax charge amount is then:

In the SII incoming invoices book (Libro de Facturas Recibidas), retailer invoices under Regime 15 have specific requirements:

  • The invoice must be classified with Clave de Régimen = "15" in the <ClaveRegimenEspecialOTrascendencia> field of the received invoices XML.
  • Instead of the standard <CuotaSoportada> (tax amount), the XML must use <CargaImpositivaImplicita> (Implicit Tax Charge).
  • The <TipoImpositivo> field must report the actual IGIC rate, not the coefficient.

 

The Coefficient (K) — How SAP Calculates the IGIC Rate

In SAP's SII solution, the system expects tax codes to be configured with the coefficient (K), not the actual IGIC rate. SAP then back-calculates the real IGIC rate using the inverse formula: IGIC Rate = Coefficient (K) / 0.70

This calculated IGIC rate is what SAP sends to the Tax Agency in the <TipoImpositivo> field.

 

The Problem

The error 1166: "El valor del campo TipoImpositivo no está incluido en la lista de valores permitidos" ("The value of the TipoImpositivo field is not included in the list of allowed values") occurs when customers configure the tax code with the actual IGIC rate instead of the coefficient (K).
 
For example:  suppose a customer creates a tax code for a retailer company supplier with the standard IGIC rate of 7%:
  1. Tax code is configured with rate: 7% (incorrect — this should be the coefficient 4.9%)
  2. SAP's SII process applies the back-calculation: 7% / 0.70 = 10%
  3. SAP sends <TipoImpositivo>10.00</TipoImpositivo> to the Tax Agency
  4. The Canary Islands Tax Agency rejects the submission: 10% is not a valid IGIC rate
  5. ❌ Error 1166 is returned

Root Cause: The tax code percentage was set to the IGIC rate (7%) instead of the coefficient K (4.9%). SAP's conversion formula produces an invalid rate (10%), which is rejected by the Tax Agency.

The Solution

Configure your tax codes for retailer company suppliers (Régimen 15) using the coefficient (K), not the IGIC tax rate. For example:

 

This means that you need to identify which tax codes will be used for incoming invoices from retailer company suppliers in the Canary Islands and use  the coefficient (K) in those tax codes

  • For IGIC 3% → set the tax code rate to 2.1%
  • For IGIC 5% → set the tax code rate to 3.5%
  • For IGIC 7% → set the tax code rate to 4.9%
  • ...

When properly configured with coefficient K = 4.9% for a 7% IGIC operation, the SII XML for a received invoice under Régimen 15 should look like this:

<FacturaRecibida>
  <TipoFactura>F1</TipoFactura>
  <ClaveRegimenEspecialOTrascendencia>15</ClaveRegimenEspecialOTrascendencia>
  <DesgloseFactura>
    <DesgloseIGIC>
      <DetalleIGIC>
        <TipoImpositivo>7.00</TipoImpositivo>
        <BaseImponible>1000.00</BaseImponible>
        <CargaImpositivaImplicita>49.00</CargaImpositivaImplicita>
      </DetalleIGIC>
    </DesgloseIGIC>
  </DesgloseFactura>
</FacturaRecibida>

Notice:

  • <ClaveRegimenEspecialOTrascendencia> = 15 (retailer company)
  • <TipoImpositivo> = 7.00 (the real IGIC rate, correctly derived from K)
  • <CargaImpositivaImplicita> is used instead of <CuotaSoportada>
  • Implicit tax charge: 1000 × 4.9% = 49.00

 

Further Reading

In case of additional questions, you can visit the SII help documentation in the following links:

  • 📘 Electronic Tax Register Books with SII in S/4HANA Public Cloud
  • 📘 Electronic Tax Register Books with SII in S/4HANA Private Cloud
  • 📘 Electronic Tax Register Books with SII in ERP
  • 📝 SII Tip for Spain #7: Error 1166 and Tax Procedures
  • SAP Note 2753243 - Canary Island: How to convert "Implicit IGIC" included in prices into some posted tax line item

 

Disclaimer

This blog post reflects the author's interpretation of Spanish legal requirements and SAP system behavior as of the publication date. It is intended for informational purposes only and does not constitute legal advice. For specific guidance, consult your legal or tax advisor and refer to official SAP documentation.

The links to external sources in this blog may be modified at any time externally by the owner without previous notice.

entry.title: EDOC_ADAPTOR~SET_OUTPUT_DATA, how to change content for KSeF 2.0 Poland FA(3)
entry.content:
eDocuments or eInvoices are common these days. In many countries, companies needs to send invoice related data to government portals due to legal requirements. Even if the target formats are standardized (at least provided by governments), companies might need to change the XML content before sending out.

This blog explains how to do this for Poland in KSeF FA(3) format

SAP provides a BAdI named EDOC_ADAPTOR, which defines a method SET_OUTPUT_DATA. This method can be implemented to change the content before sending the XML document. This BAdI can be implemented for any country and there are many blogs and other resources available explaining on how to do so.

So why do I need to write the n+1 instruction on this?

What's the issue here?

This week, I was challenged with changing the content of eDocuments related to Poland. I already did this in past for other countries, so I felt comfortable in doing this. Create a BAdI Implementation, filtered by country PL, change content and I'm done.

Method SET_OUTPUT_DATA of BAdI provides a changing parameter CS_OUTPUT_DATA. This is pre-filled with the content generated by the eDoc framework.

If one need to tweak the data, CS_OUTPUT_DATA is the one which needs to be change, right?
Right!

But you might notice, that CS_OUTPUT_DATA seems to be somewhat of empty in case of KSeF. It's a deep structure with nested tables, but everything is initial. If you try visualize content of XML in EDOC_COCKPIT, XML shows up. 
Why can you visualize data in EDOC_COKPIT if CS_OUTPUT_DATA is not filled at all? How can I change data, if system doesn't provide the outcome of framework?

CS_OUTPUT_DATA structure is empty

Solution

There is an OSS note https://me.sap.com/notes/3346323/E#Topic_F, which describes the issue and provides a small hint on what you need to do. Surprisingly, the note was released on 27th March 2026. As of this writing, it has been released TODAY. It feels like I'm the first person coming across this issue

 

The content is available in CS_OUTPUT_DATA-INVOICE-INVOICE_CONTENT but in an encoded manner.

How to change the content

  1. fetch data from CS_OUTPUT_DATA-INVOICE-INVOICE_CONTENT
  2. decode base64 content of 1 into XSTRING
  3. convert XSTRING into ABAP structure. XSTRING here is the XML itself
  4. change your data in ABAP structure
  5. convert ABAP structure back into XML XSTRING representation
  6. encode XString into base64 format
  7. return this as CS_OUTPUT_DATA-INVOICE-INVOICE_CONTENT
  METHOD if_edoc_adaptor~set_output_data.
    DATA io_edocument   TYPE REF TO cl_edocument.

    DATA xml_content_in TYPE xstring.
    DATA output_data    TYPE edo_pl_faktura3.

    FIELD-SYMBOLS <output>                TYPE edo_pl_send_invoice_type.
   
    " read data provided by framework
    ASSIGN cs_output_data TO <output>.
    DATA(edoc_content) = <output>-invoice-invoice_content.

    " decode Base64 encoded FA(3) Invoice to XString
    CALL FUNCTION 'SCMS_BASE64_DECODE_STR'
      EXPORTING  input  = edoc_content
*                 unescape = 'X'
      IMPORTING  output = xml_content_in
      EXCEPTIONS failed = 1
                 OTHERS = 2.
    IF sy-subrc <> 0.
*    MESSAGE ID SY-MSGID TYPE SY-MSGTY NUMBER SY-MSGNO
*      WITH SY-MSGV1 SY-MSGV2 SY-MSGV3 SY-MSGV4.
    ENDIF.

    TRY.
        " convert XML XString into ABAP structure
        cl_proxy_xml_transform=>xml_xstring_to_abap( EXPORTING ddic_type = 'EDO_PL_FAKTURA3'
                                                               xml       = xml_content_in
                                                     IMPORTING abap_data = output_data ).
      CATCH cx_proxy_fault.
      CATCH cx_transformation_error.

    ENDTRY.

    " CHANGE DATA HERE
    " add your logic here
    " CHANGE DATA HERE

    " convert new output data to XML XString
    DATA(xml_content_out) = cl_proxy_xml_transform=>abap_to_xml_xstring( abap_data = output_data
                                                                         ddic_type = 'EDO_PL_FAKTURA3' ).

    " encode XML XString to Base64 and use it as new output data
    CALL FUNCTION 'SCMS_BASE64_ENCODE_STR'
      EXPORTING input  = xml_content_out
      IMPORTING output = <output>-invoice-invoice_content.

  ENDMETHOD.

I found no resources about this issue and only one OSS note. I leave my solution here hoping someone else who comes across this situation will benefit. I'm glad if this saves you some hours of reading and debugging

entry.title: SAP Document and Reporting Compliance UAE: Electronic Invoice in Utilities using Extensibility
entry.content:
You want to enable your system to create electronic documents for invoices posted in Utilities using SAP Document and Reporting Compliance. In this blog, we’ll show you an example on how this can be achieved using extensibility.  

Before you begin ensure that you have implemented the SAP Notes listed in 3705171 - Electronic Document Processing for United Arab Emirates - Peppol: Implementation Overview. You can also refer the SAP Help Portal for more information, see Electronic Document Processing.
Ensure you have also implemented the SAP Notes listed in 2736880 - eDocument EU PEPPOL: Overview note for IS-U.

Steps to Create Electronic Documents

  • Create eDocument Class
    1. Create 'ZCL_EDOCUMENT_AE_ISU' eDocument class by inheriting CL_EDOCUMENT_EU_ISU class.
    2. Add the CONSTRUCTOR method in the ZCL_EDOCUMENT_AE_ISU eDocument class to map the 'Z' eDocument IS-U class name to the field ms_edocument-edocument_class. 
    3. Redefine the method DETERMINE_PROCESS to assign the processes AEINV and AEINVIN.
    4. Use the table TFK003EDOC to configure eDocument type against the accounting document type.
    Example:

    Country/Region 

    Application Area 

    Document Type 

    eDocument Type 

    IN 

    Utility Company 

    FA 

    AE_INV 

     5. Add the class CL_EDOC_PROCESS as Friends of 'ZCL_EDOCUMENT_AE_ISU' eDocument class. 

    Sample Code for CONSTRUCTOR and DETERMINE_PROCESS method:  
    Constructor method signature: 

     


METHOD constructor.
 super->constructor( io_source_data                 = io_source_data
                      iv_edoc_guid                   = iv_edoc_guid
                      iv_land                        = iv_land
                      iv_update_task                 = iv_update_task
                      iv_generic_badi_filter_adaptor = iv_generic_badi_filter_adaptor
                      iv_simulate_for_email_option = iv_simulate_for_email_option ).

  ms_edocument-land                   = 'AE'.
  ms_edocument-edocument_class        = 'ZCL_EDOCUMENT_AE_ISU'.
ENDMETHOD.
METHOD determine_process.
    CASE ms_edocument-edoc_type.
      WHEN 'AE_INVIN'.
        ms_edocument-process = 'AEINVIN'.
        ms_edocument-process_version = '0001'.
      WHEN OTHERS.
        CASE ms_edocument-interface_type.
          WHEN 'AIF_PROXY' OR 'STANDARD'.
            ms_edocument-process = 'AEINV'.
*           reference process is 'EUINV'
            ms_edocument-process_version = '0001'.
          WHEN OTHERS.
            MESSAGE e162(edocument) INTO gv_error_txt.
            raise_edoc_exception( ).
        ENDCASE.
    ENDCASE.
ENDMETHOD.

 

  • Create Factory Class
    1. Create a 'Z' factory class to get the instance of eDocument IS-U class by inheriting CL_EDOC_FACTORY class and redefine its method CREATE_EDOC_INSTANCE. 
    Example: ZCL_EDOC_FACTORY_AE_ISU
    2. Configure this 'Z' eDocument factory class in the view EDOFACTORYV using the transaction SM30.

    Source Type  

    Factory Class  

    ISU_INVOIC  

    ‘Z’ factory class name (your factory class that you just created)  


    Sample Code:
METHOD create_edoc_instances.

    DATA: lv_land_iso      TYPE edoc_land,
          lv_error_txt     TYPE string,
          ls_source_header TYPE edoc_src_header,
          lo_edocument     TYPE REF TO zcl_edocument_ae_isu.

    CLEAR: lv_land_iso,
           lv_error_txt,
           ls_source_header,
           lo_edocument,
           et_edocument_obj.

* Country
    ls_source_header = io_source->get_header( ).
    CALL FUNCTION 'COUNTRY_CODE_SAP_TO_ISO'
      EXPORTING
        sap_code  = ls_source_header-land
      IMPORTING
        iso_code  = lv_land_iso
      EXCEPTIONS
        not_found = 1
        OTHERS    = 2.
    IF sy-subrc <> 0.
      MESSAGE e881(b1) WITH ls_source_header-land INTO lv_error_txt.
    ENDIF.
    IF lv_land_iso <> 'AE'.
      RETURN.
    ENDIF.

    CREATE OBJECT lo_edocument 
      EXPORTING
        io_source_data = io_source
        iv_update_task = iv_update_task.

    APPEND lo_edocument TO et_edocument_obj.

  ENDMETHOD.

  • Mapping Class
    1. We have a generic mapping class CL_EDOC_MAP_UBL_ISU. If you need to enhance the mapping logic, you can use the EDOC_ADAPTOR BAdI's method SET_OUTPUT_DATA. For more details, refer to Enhancements for Electronic Documents.
